006 * "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with the

008 *

010 *

016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.http.header;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
022
023import java.util.*;
024import java.util.function.*;
025
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.http.*;
027
028
029/**
030 * Represents an HTTP <c>Content-Disposition</c> header.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * Content disposition (inline / attachment / filename).
034 *
035 * <p>
036 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
037 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
038 *
039 * @since 9.2.1
040 */
041@SuppressWarnings({
042   "java:S1192" // Duplicated "value" literals are HTTP header component keys; a constant would obscure the header grammar.
043})
044public class ContentDisposition extends HttpStringRangesHeader {
045
046   public static final String NAME = "Content-Disposition";
047
048   public ContentDisposition(String value) {
049      super(NAME, value);
050   }
051
052   public ContentDisposition(StringRanges value) {
053      super(NAME, value);
054   }
055
056   private ContentDisposition(Supplier<?> supplier, int lazyMode) {
057      super(NAME, supplier, lazyMode);
058   }
059
060   public static ContentDisposition of(String value) {
061      return new ContentDisposition(value);
062   }
063
064   public static ContentDisposition of(StringRanges value) {
065      return new ContentDisposition(value);
066   }
067
068   public static ContentDisposition ofLazyWire(Supplier<String> supplier) {
069      return new ContentDisposition(supplier, LAZY_WIRE_STRING);
070   }
071
072   public static ContentDisposition ofLazyParsed(Supplier<StringRanges> supplier) {
073      return new ContentDisposition(supplier, LAZY_STRING_RANGES);
074   }
075
076   /**
077    * Returns a {@code Content-Disposition} header value of the form {@code attachment; filename="<filename>"} with
078    * the file name properly escaped (backslashes and double-quotes are backslash-escaped).
079    *
080    * @param filename The attachment filename. Must not be {@code null}, blank, or contain CR/LF characters.
081    * @return A new {@code ContentDisposition} header.
082    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code filename} is invalid.
083    */
084   public static ContentDisposition attachment(String filename) {
085      if (isBlank(filename))
086         throw iaex("Attachment filename must not be null or blank.");
087      for (var i = 0; i < filename.length(); i++) {
088         var c = filename.charAt(i);
089         if (c == '\r' || c == '\n')
090            throw iaex("Attachment filename must not contain CR or LF characters.");
091      }
092      var sb = new StringBuilder(filename.length() + 8);
093      for (var i = 0; i < filename.length(); i++) {
094         var c = filename.charAt(i);
095         if (c == '\\' || c == '"')
096            sb.append('\\');
097         sb.append(c);
098      }
099      return ContentDisposition.of("attachment; filename=\"" + sb + "\"");
100   }
101
102   /**
103    * Creates a new empty {@link Builder}.
104    *
105    * @return A new builder. Never <jk>null</jk>.
106    */
107   public static Builder create() {
108      return new Builder();
109   }
110
111   //------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
112   // Inner types
113   //------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
114
115   /**
116    * Fluent builder for assembling an HTTP <c>Content-Disposition</c> header value.
117    *
118    * <p>
119    * Composes the disposition-type and parameters defined by
120    * <a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6266'>RFC 6266</a> into the
121    * <c>disposition-type; param=value; ...</c> wire format.  Typed setters cover the common parameters and apply the
122    * correct rendering (quoted-string for textual params, a bare number for <c>size</c>, and a raw
123    * <a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5987'>RFC 5987</a> ext-value for <c>filename*</c>);
124    * {@link #param(String, String)} is a generic escape hatch.  Setters overwrite a previously-set parameter of the
125    * same name while preserving its original position.
126    *
127    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
128    * <p class='bjava'>
129    *    String <jv>value</jv> = ContentDisposition.<jsm>create</jsm>()
130    *       .attachment()
131    *       .filename(<js>"genome.jpeg"</js>)
132    *       .size(12345)
133    *       .build();
134    *    <jc>// =&gt; "attachment; filename=\"genome.jpeg\"; size=12345"</jc>
135    * </p>
136    *
137    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
138    *    <li class='jc'>{@link ContentDisposition}
139    *    <li class='extlink'><a class='doclink' href='https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6266'>RFC 6266 - Use of the Content-Disposition Header Field</a>
140    * </ul>
141    *
142    * @since 10.0.0
143    */
144   public static class Builder {
145
146      private String type;
147      private final Map<String,String> params = new LinkedHashMap<>();
148
149      /**
150       * Sets the disposition-type (e.g. <js>"inline"</js>, <js>"attachment"</js>, <js>"form-data"</js>).
151       *
152       * @param value The disposition-type. Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
153       * @return This object.
154       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
155       */
156      public Builder type(String value) {
157         assertArgNotNull("value", value);
158         var v = value.trim();
159         if (v.isEmpty())
160            throw iaex("disposition-type must not be blank");
161         type = v;
162         return this;
163      }
164
165      /**
166       * Sets the disposition-type to <c>inline</c>.
167       *
168       * @return This object.
169       */
170      public Builder inline() {
171         return type("inline");
172      }
173
174      /**
175       * Sets the disposition-type to <c>attachment</c>.
176       *
177       * @return This object.
178       */
179      public Builder attachment() {
180         return type("attachment");
181      }
182
183      /**
184       * Sets the disposition-type to <c>form-data</c>.
185       *
186       * @return This object.
187       */
188      public Builder formData() {
189         return type("form-data");
190      }
191
192      /**
193       * Sets the <c>name</c> parameter (rendered as a quoted-string).
194       *
195       * @param value The field name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
196       * @return This object.
197       */
198      public Builder name(String value) {
199         return quotedParam("name", value);
200      }
201
202      /**
203       * Sets the <c>filename</c> parameter (rendered as a quoted-string with backslash/quote escaping).
204       *
205       * @param value The filename. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
206       * @return This object.
207       */
208      public Builder filename(String value) {
209         return quotedParam("filename", value);
210      }
211
212      /**
213       * Sets the <c>filename*</c> parameter (rendered as a raw RFC 5987 ext-value, unquoted).
214       *
215       * @param value The encoded ext-value (e.g. <js>"UTF-8''%e2%82%ac%20rates"</js>). Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
216       * @return This object.
217       */
218      public Builder filenameExt(String value) {
219         return tokenParam("filename*", value);
220      }
221
222      /**
223       * Sets the <c>creation-date</c> parameter (rendered as a quoted HTTP-date).
224       *
225       * @param value The creation date. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
226       * @return This object.
227       */
228      public Builder creationDate(String value) {
229         return quotedParam("creation-date", value);
230      }
231
232      /**
233       * Sets the <c>modification-date</c> parameter (rendered as a quoted HTTP-date).
234       *
235       * @param value The modification date. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
236       * @return This object.
237       */
238      public Builder modificationDate(String value) {
239         return quotedParam("modification-date", value);
240      }
241
242      /**
243       * Sets the <c>read-date</c> parameter (rendered as a quoted HTTP-date).
244       *
245       * @param value The read date. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
246       * @return This object.
247       */
248      public Builder readDate(String value) {
249         return quotedParam("read-date", value);
250      }
251
252      /**
253       * Sets the <c>size</c> parameter (rendered as a bare number).
254       *
255       * @param value The size in bytes. Must be non-negative.
256       * @return This object.
257       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code value} is negative.
258       */
259      public Builder size(long value) {
260         if (value < 0)
261            throw iaex("size must be non-negative: {0}", value);
262         return tokenParam("size", Long.toString(value));
263      }
264
265      /**
266       * Generic escape hatch for setting any parameter by name, rendered as a quoted-string.
267       *
268       * <p>
269       * Replaces any previously-registered value for the same parameter while preserving its original position.
270       *
271       * @param name The parameter name. Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
272       * @param value The parameter value (quoted automatically). Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
273       * @return This object.
274       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code name} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
275       */
276      public Builder param(String name, String value) {
277         return quotedParam(name, value);
278      }
279
280      private Builder quotedParam(String name, String value) {
281         assertArgNotNull("value", value);
282         params.put(cleanName(name), q(value));
283         return this;
284      }
285
286      private Builder tokenParam(String name, String value) {
287         assertArgNotNull("value", value);
288         params.put(cleanName(name), value);
289         return this;
290      }
291
292      private static String cleanName(String name) {
293         assertArgNotNull("name", name);
294         var n = name.trim();
295         if (n.isEmpty())
296            throw iaex("parameter name must not be blank");
297         return n;
298      }
299
300      private static String q(String value) {
301         var sb = new StringBuilder(value.length() + 2);
302         sb.append('"');
303         for (var i = 0; i < value.length(); i++) {
304            var c = value.charAt(i);
305            if (c == '\\' || c == '"')
306               sb.append('\\');
307            sb.append(c);
308         }
309         sb.append('"');
310         return sb.toString();
311      }
312
313      /**
314       * Builds the rendered <c>Content-Disposition</c> header value.
315       *
316       * <p>
317       * Returns an empty string when no disposition-type or parameters have been registered.
318       *
319       * @return The header value. Never <jk>null</jk>.
320       */
321      public String build() {
322         var parts = new ArrayList<String>(params.size() + 1);
323         if (type != null)
324            parts.add(type);
325         params.forEach((k, v) -> parts.add(k + "=" + v));
326         return String.join("; ", parts);
327      }
328
329      /**
330       * Builds a {@link ContentDisposition} header bean directly from this builder.
331       *
332       * @return A {@link ContentDisposition} carrying {@link #build()}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
333       */
334      public ContentDisposition toHeader() {
335         return ContentDisposition.of(build());
336      }
337
338      @Override
339      public String toString() {
340         return build();
341      }
342   }
343}
